steps.title: Identify relevant totals
steps.content: The table shows 415 teens talk on a cell phone daily and 799 teens total.

steps.title: Compute probability and select choice
steps.content:
Probability = 415/799 = $$\frac{415}{799}$$.
That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. If one of the teens surveyed is selected at random, the probability that the teen talks on a cell phone daily is equal to the quotient of the total number of teens who reported that they talk on a cell phone daily, 415, and the total number of teens surveyed, 799. Therefore, this probability is equal to $415 / 799$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This fraction represents the probability of selecting at random any one of the 799 teens surveyed. Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual errors. Choice D is incorrect. This fraction represents the probability of selecting at random one of the 799 teens surveyed who doesn't talk on a cell phone daily.
